actions.value: Blockchains are considered pseudonymous because they are public ledgers but without human-readable names. In some cases, chain-analysis is possible to 'follow the money' and trace it to someone's centralized exchange account; in which case, you can attempt to recover assets with help from the authorities working directly with the exchange.

Blockchains are considered pseudonymous because they are public ledgers but without human-readable names. In some cases, chain-analysis is possible to 'follow the money' and trace it to someone's centralized exchange account; in which case, you can attempt to recover assets with help from the authorities working directly with the exchange.
